name: Lalo Schifrin
description: Argentine-American pianist, composer, arranger and conductor (1932–2025)
abstract: Boris Claudio "Lalo" Schifrin (Spanish pronunciation: [ˈlalo ˈʃifɾin]; June 21, 1932 – June 26, 2025) was an Argentine and American pianist, composer, arranger and conductor. Initially prominent as a jazz composer, he was best known for his large body of film and television scores, which incorporates jazz and Latin American musical eleme…

about_quotes.evidence: That was when I did the album “Dingo” with Miles. ... So Michel Legrand says to me, “Chuck come in here, I want you to hear — you have to be this person. about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: Lalo Schifrin was the musical arranger of that program and the arranger of my recording called Conga Soul. There are several numbers there composed by him and me.

about_quotes.speaker: Pierre Boussaguet
about_quotes.evidence: Lalo calls me regularly and we became good friends. We still communicate.

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Mingus
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: Schiffer. (Context: Interviewee Charles Mingus is asked about a piano player used to be with Dizzy Gillespie, identified as Lalo Schifrin, but Mingus does not speak or confirm a personal relationship in the provided text.)

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: Most of the composers in the sixties for Universal Studios especially were primarily jazz musicians. Benny Golson, Benny Carter, Quincy Jones, Oliver Nelson, Lalo Schifrin...
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
